title: Compensated Absences (Vacation Accrual)
event: By year-end, Hollis Co.'s employees have earned 20 employees × 5 unused vacation days × 8 hours × $25 per hour of vested vacation pay, to be taken next year. Prepare the accrual entry.

lines.label: vacation earned this year
lines.why: The vacation was EARNED by this year's service — the expense belongs here, not when the beach trip happens.
lines.trap: Accrual requires: services rendered, rights vest or accumulate, payment probable, amount estimable — all four hold.

lines.label: owed for future absences
lines.why: The obligation to pay for next year's time off.
lines.trap: SICK pay that doesn't vest is the standard exception — accumulating-but-nonvesting sick days MAY be accrued but usually aren't.

memorize.body: Four accrual criteria: services rendered, rights vest or accumulate, probable payment, estimable amount. Vacation almost always qualifies; nonvesting sick pay is the carve-out.
If pay RATES rise before the vacation is taken, the difference is expensed in the year of payment — the accrual uses current rates.

questions.prompt: 20 employees × 5 days × 8 hours × $25, vested and unused. Vacation expense accrued THIS year = ?

distractors.feedback: Waiting until the vacations are taken is cash-basis thinking. Vested, earned, probable, estimable → accrue 20,000 now.

build.scaffold: Earned this year, paid next year — the accrual family's signature. Rate changes wait for the payment year.
